title: Avoid repeated refs-vec allocation in test evaluators
description: eval_header_test, eval_address_test, and eval_envelope_test all start with `let refs: Vec<&Form> = forms.iter().collect();` and then pass that Vec through three chained extract_* functions, each of which returns a new Vec<&Form>. This is three allocations plus the initial collect per test invocation. The extract_* functions could take `&[Form]` directly (iterating with .iter()) and return a filtered SmallVec or just iterate twice. A cleaner alternative: replace the three extract_* functions with a single `extract_test_args(forms: &[Form]) -> TestArgs` struct that fills MatchType, Comparator, AddressPart, names, and keys in one pass, eliminating all intermediate Vecs. The current pattern also means the signature `fn extract_match_type<'a>(forms: &[&'a Form])` is unusual — it takes a slice of references to Form rather than a slice of Form, which is a type-level smell.
